name: Shell Excellence
description: Ten-gate Shell/Bash enforcement: architecture, performance, portability, safety, testing, CI, documentation, UX design, release readiness, truth. ShellCheck zero warnings, set -euo pipefail mandatory, POSIX-portable, idempotent scripts, zero unquoted expansions, zero hardcoded secrets, low memory, low CPU.

phases.name: Architecture & Idioms
phases.principle: Is the structure sound, modular, and idiomatic?

delegates.task_template: Enforce modular, idempotent shell script architecture for: ${goal}. Refactor large scripts (>200 lines) into modular functions with single responsibility. Every script MUST be idempotent: safe to run multiple times without side effects (use mkdir -p not mkdir, use cp -n or check-before-write, guard destructive ops). Enforce: one main() function as entry point, helper functions defined before main, local variables inside functions (local keyword), no global mutable state. Shebang MUST be #!/usr/bin/env bash (not #!/cli/bin/bash). Enforce set -euo pipefail at the top of every script (after shebang). Flag: scripts >300 lines without functions, nested logic >3 levels deep, functions >50 lines, scripts that source more than 3 files. Enforce lib/ directory for shared functions, cli/bin/ for entry points. Score Tech Debt (target 10/10) and Modularity (target 100/100).
verifies: set -euo pipefail at top of every script

delegates.task_template: Enforce modern shell idioms across all scripts for: ${goal}. Flag: backtick command substitution (use $()), test with [ ] (use [[ ]]), seq in for loops (use {1..10} or C-style for), cat file | grep (useless use of cat), echo with escape sequences (use printf), manual parsing of command output (use built-in parameter expansion), eval usage, temporary files without cleanup (use trap for EXIT), heredocs without quoting delimiter when no expansion needed, arithmetic with expr (use $(())). Enforce: ${variable:-default} for defaults, ${variable:?error} for required vars, [[ -f ]] over test -f, read -r always, IFS handling for word splitting, arrays for lists (not space-separated strings), mapfile/readarray for line processing, process substitution <() over temp files, declare -r for constants, readonly for immutable vars.
verifies: printf over echo for formatted output

delegates.task_template: Conduct deep research on current shell scripting best practices for: ${goal}. Research: POSIX sh compatibility matrix vs bash-specific features, ShellCheck rule categories and severity levels, current bash 5.x features (associative arrays, nameref, loadable builtins), portable alternatives to common bashisms, cross-platform differences (macOS default shell is zsh, Linux is bash, BSD vs GNU coreutils), structured output patterns (JSON from shell via jq), modern argument parsing (getopts vs getopt vs manual), process management best practices (trap, wait, job control), container-friendly scripting (Alpine/busybox ash compatibility). Cross-validate findings against Bash manual, POSIX spec, ShellCheck wiki, and Google Shell Style Guide. Flag deprecated patterns.

Audit shell script file organization for: ${goal}. Verify every script has a header comment block explaining purpose, usage, and dependencies. Verify directory structure: cli/bin/ for executable entry points, lib/ for sourced libraries, tests/ for test scripts mirroring cli/bin/ and lib/. Remove orphaned scripts not referenced anywhere. Enforce consistent file naming (kebab-case, no .sh extension on cli/bin/ executables, .sh on lib/ sources). Verify executable permissions set correctly (chmod +x on cli/bin/, not on lib/). Clean up backup files (*~, *.bak, *.swp). Verify .editorconfig sets shell file defaults (indent_style=space, indent_size=2). Verify Makefile or task runner documents available commands.
verifies: every script has header comment block
cli/bin/ for executables, lib/ for libraries
Makefile or task runner documents commands

phases.checkpoint: Modular, idempotent, set -euo pipefail, modern idioms, best practices researched, files organized
phases.failure_message: Architecture gate failed. Add set -euo pipefail, refactor into functions, and fix idioms.

phases.name: Performance & Resources
phases.principle: Is every operation fast, lean, and resource-efficient?

delegates.task_template: Audit shell script performance for: ${goal}. Eliminate unnecessary subshells and external command forks: use bash built-ins (parameter expansion, arithmetic, string manipulation) over sed/awk/cut for simple operations. Flag: command substitution in loops (fork per iteration), cat | while read (use while read < file or process substitution), grep | wc -l (use grep -c), find | xargs without -print0/-0 (unsafe and slow). Enforce: parallel execution where independent (GNU parallel, xargs -P, & with wait), bulk operations over item-by-item (find -exec + over find -exec \;), built-in string ops (${var#pattern}, ${var%pattern}, ${var//old/new}). Profile with time and bash -x. Verify no busy-wait loops (sleep in polling). Verify cleanup: temp files removed, background processes terminated, file descriptors closed. Target: low CPU, low memory, minimal fork count.
verifies: built-ins used over external commands where possible
no command substitution in tight loops
low CPU and minimal fork count

delegates.task_template: Audit shell script external dependency health for: ${goal}. Verify every external command used is checked for existence at script start (command -v or type). Document minimum required versions of external tools (jq, curl, git, etc.). Flag: dependency on GNU-specific flags without checking (--long-options not in BSD), tools not available in minimal containers (Alpine/busybox), version-specific behavior without guards. Verify: no reliance on specific PATH ordering, no hardcoded tool paths (/usr/local/cli/bin/...), fallback alternatives for optional deps. Verify .tool-versions or equivalent pins versions. Keep external dependencies minimal: flag scripts requiring >10 external tools. Verify no dev-only tools in production scripts.
verifies: no GNU-specific flags without BSD fallback
no dev-only tools in production scripts

phases.checkpoint: Built-ins preferred, forks minimized, dependencies checked and portable
phases.failure_message: Performance gate failed. Excessive forks, missing dependency checks, or GNU-only assumptions detected.

phases.name: Portability & Platform Parity
phases.principle: Does it build and pass identically on every platform?

delegates.task_template: Verify cross-platform portability for: ${goal}. Scripts MUST execute correctly on macOS (zsh/bash 3.2+), Linux (bash 4+/5+), and WSL without modification. Test on: macOS (BSD coreutils), Ubuntu/Debian (GNU coreutils), Alpine (busybox). Flag incompatible commands: GNU sed -i vs BSD sed -i '' (use portable pattern or detect), GNU grep -P (use grep -E), readlink -f (not on macOS, use realpath or manual resolution), stat format differences, date format differences, mktemp differences. Verify: no bash 4+ features if targeting macOS stock bash (associative arrays, nameref, mapfile), or document minimum bash version requirement. Verify line endings: scripts use LF (not CRLF). Verify locale handling: LC_ALL=C for consistent sort/grep. Flag any uname checks without corresponding platform-specific code paths.
verifies: BSD vs GNU coreutils differences handled
no bash 4+ features without version guard

phases.checkpoint: Cross-platform parity verified on macOS, Linux, WSL with BSD and GNU coreutils
phases.failure_message: Portability gate failed. Platform-specific commands or bash version incompatibilities detected.

phases.name: Safety & Security
phases.principle: Is the code safe and the supply chain clean?

delegates.task_template: Security audit of all shell scripts for: ${goal}. Scan for: unquoted variable expansions (word splitting and glob injection: $var instead of "$var"), format string vulnerabilities (NSLog/printf with user input as format: printf $user_input instead of printf '%s' "$user_input"), command injection via eval/backticks with user input, unsafe temporary files (predictable names in /tmp without mktemp), TOCTOU race conditions (test then act without locks), hardcoded secrets (passwords, API keys, tokens in scripts), curl without certificate validation (--insecure/-k), wget without HTTPS, sourcing untrusted files, PATH manipulation attacks. Verify: all variables double-quoted ("$var"), all user input sanitized before use in commands, mktemp for temp files, trap for cleanup, no secrets in scripts (use env vars or secrets manager). Flag severity and provide remediation.
verifies: no curl --insecure or wget without HTTPS
all user input sanitized before command use

delegates.task_template: Audit shell script supply chain and operational security for: ${goal}. Verify: scripts do not download and execute remote code (curl | bash pattern) without checksum verification, all downloaded artifacts verified (sha256sum/shasum), GPG signatures checked where available. Verify no scripts run with unnecessary privileges (no sudo without justification). Audit: file permissions on sensitive scripts (no world-writable), umask set for created files, credentials passed via environment variables not command-line arguments (visible in ps). Verify .gitignore excludes .env, credentials, private keys. Verify no API keys or tokens committed in script history. Check for scripts that modify system state without confirmation. Classify threat level using S0-S3 severity.
verifies: no curl | bash without checksum verification
credentials via env vars (not CLI args)

phases.checkpoint: Zero unquoted expansions, zero hardcoded secrets, supply chain verified
phases.failure_message: Safety gate failed. Unquoted variables, hardcoded secrets, or insecure downloads detected.

phases.name: Testing & Coverage
phases.principle: Is every code path tested with evidence?

delegates.task_template: Enforce comprehensive shell script testing for: ${goal}. Test with bats-core (Bash Automated Testing System): every function in lib/ has corresponding .bats test file, every cli/bin/ script has integration test. Achieve function-level coverage of all exported functions. Verify mock discipline: external commands mocked in tests (bats-mock or function overrides), filesystem operations use temp directories (setup/teardown). Test error paths: verify set -e catches failures, verify pipefail catches pipe errors, verify unbound variable detection (set -u). Test edge cases: empty inputs, spaces in filenames, special characters, Unicode filenames, symlinks, missing dependencies (command -v checks). Verify tests do not rely on swizzling or host state. Verify idempotency: run each script twice, verify identical outcome. Test signal handling: SIGTERM/SIGINT trigger cleanup.
verifies: bats-core tests for every lib/ function
integration tests for every cli/bin/ script
edge cases tested (spaces, special chars, Unicode)
no test reliance on host state

phases.checkpoint: bats-core tests complete, error paths verified, idempotency proven, signals handled
phases.failure_message: Testing gate failed. Missing tests, untested error paths, or idempotency violations detected.

phases.name: CI & Automation
phases.principle: Does CI enforce every standard automatically?

delegates.task_template: Enforce full shell script CI pipeline for: ${goal}. ShellCheck on ALL .sh and shebang-detected scripts: treat all warnings as errors (shellcheck -S error), enable all optional checks (shellcheck -o all). shfmt for formatting (indent=2, binary-next-line, case-indent, space-redirects). bats-core test suite with TAP output. Pre-commit hooks: shellcheck, shfmt --diff. CI matrix: macOS-latest + Ubuntu-latest + Alpine (busybox) at minimum. Verify set -euo pipefail present (custom lint or grep check). Verify no hardcoded secrets (gitleaks or trufflehog in pipeline). Verify executable permissions correct. SLO enforcement: shellcheck <5s, tests <60s. No '|| true' on any step. Nightly job for ShellCheck version updates and bash nightly testing.

phases.checkpoint: CI pipeline enforces ShellCheck, shfmt, bats, secrets scanning, cross-platform matrix
phases.failure_message: CI gate failed. ShellCheck not enforced, missing tests, or incomplete matrix.

phases.name: Documentation
phases.principle: Is every public API documented with runnable examples?

delegates.task_template: Audit and perfect all shell script documentation for: ${goal}. Every script MUST have a usage header: script name, one-line description, USAGE section with synopsis, FLAGS/OPTIONS with description and defaults, ARGUMENTS with description, ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES used, DEPENDENCIES required, EXAMPLES with runnable commands, EXIT CODES documented. Verify --help and -h flags print usage and exit 0. Verify usage shown on invalid arguments (exit 2). Library files (lib/) MUST document every exported function with: description, arguments, return value, side effects, example. README MUST include: project description, prerequisites, installation, quickstart, script inventory with descriptions, contributing guide. Generate man pages or help2man output for primary scripts.
verifies: README with prerequisites and script inventory
man pages or help2man for primary scripts

delegates.task_template: Audit all shell script documentation and user-facing content for brand voice compliance for: ${goal}. Enforce the Euxis Standard: authoritative, concise, imperative mood. Cut all hedging ('you might', 'it seems', 'this should'), filler ('basically', 'simply', 'just'), passive voice, and corporate jargon ('leverage', 'utilize', 'robust', 'facilitate'). Verify: short declarative sentences (8-15 words), benefit before mechanism, active voice always, one idea per sentence. Audit usage headers, --help output, error messages, README, and inline comments. Output word count reduction and principle violations found.
verifies: brand voice audit complete with metrics

phases.checkpoint: Every script documented with usage/help, library functions annotated, brand voice enforced
phases.failure_message: Documentation gate failed. Missing usage headers, undocumented functions, or voice violations detected.

phases.name: API Design & UX
phases.principle: Is the API ergonomic, discoverable, and user-friendly?

delegates.task_template: Audit shell script CLI ergonomics and user experience for: ${goal}. Verify argument parsing: getopts or manual parsing with clear --long-option support, unknown flags produce helpful error and exit 2, missing required args show usage. Verify exit codes: 0=success, 1=general error, 2=usage error, 126=not executable, 127=not found. Verify output discipline: stderr for diagnostics/progress, stdout for data/results (pipeable), NO_COLOR respected (disable ANSI if NO_COLOR set or stdout not a terminal), color used for emphasis not information. Verify interactive UX: confirmation prompts for destructive operations (with --force/--yes to skip), progress indicators for long operations, --dry-run mode for state-changing scripts, --verbose/-v for debug output, --quiet/-q for silent mode. Verify scripts work in pipes: accept stdin, produce clean stdout.
verifies: stderr for diagnostics, stdout for data
confirmation for destructive operations (--force to skip)

phases.checkpoint: CLI ergonomic, exit codes correct, pipe-friendly, destructive ops guarded
phases.failure_message: API design gate failed. Poor argument parsing, missing --dry-run, or pipe-unfriendly output detected.

phases.name: Hardening & Release
phases.principle: Is it release-ready with baselines, changelogs, and semver?

delegates.task_template: Stress test and establish performance baselines for: ${goal}. Benchmark critical scripts with hyperfine (3+ runs, warmup). Set execution time budgets per script. Test with large inputs: 10x and 100x typical workload (large file lists, many directories, big log files). Profile with bash -x and time. Verify no unbounded memory growth: large file processing uses streaming (while read line) not slurping (var=$(cat file)). Test under resource constraints: ulimit -n (file descriptors), ulimit -v (memory). Verify parallel scripts handle job limits (--jobs). Verify cleanup under stress: kill script mid-execution, verify no orphan processes or temp files. Target: predictable execution time, linear scaling, clean abort, low memory, low CPU.
verifies: clean temp file cleanup on kill

delegates.task_template: Perform exhaustive regression analysis for: ${goal}. Verify CI catches: ShellCheck regressions (new warnings), test regressions (bats failures), portability regressions (macOS/Linux/Alpine failures), performance regressions (hyperfine comparison). Classify every change by risk: error handling modifications, argument parsing changes, file operation changes, permission changes, dependency additions. Scan for deprecated bashisms or ShellCheck-suppressed warnings older than 6 months. Verify dead code: unreachable functions, unused variables, scripts not called from anywhere. Check dev-only scripts not shipped in production. Verify CI blocks regression merges.
verifies: no dead code (unreachable functions, unused vars)

delegates.task_template: Verify release readiness for: ${goal}. Execute pre-release checklist: ShellCheck zero warnings, shfmt clean, all bats tests pass on macOS/Linux/Alpine, documentation current, no hardcoded secrets (gitleaks clean). Determine correct semantic version bump (MAJOR for breaking flag/behavior changes, MINOR for new scripts/features, PATCH for fixes). Generate changelog following Keep a Changelog format. Verify backward compatibility: renamed flags still accepted with deprecation warning, removed scripts have migration instructions. Verify installation works (curl|bash installer if applicable, brew formula, Makefile install). Draft release notes with highlights. Verify rollback plan exists. Coordinate security clearance with sentinel.

phases.checkpoint: Stress tested, regressions blocked, release-ready with semver and changelog
phases.failure_message: Hardening findings detected. Review stress tests, regressions, and release readiness.

phases.name: Final Truth Gate
phases.principle: Is every claim backed by evidence?

delegates.task_template: Final gate for: ${goal}. Validate evidence from all prior phases. Verify: architecture scores at target (Tech Debt 10/10, Modularity 100/100), set -euo pipefail on every script, modular functions, idempotent operations, modern idioms enforced, best practices researched with citations, ShellCheck zero warnings, shfmt clean, cross-platform parity on macOS/Linux/WSL/Alpine, all variables quoted, no hardcoded secrets, bats-core tests comprehensive, every script documented with usage header, --help works, exit codes correct, brand voice audit passed, CI enforces all gates, stress tests passed, regressions blocked, release readiness confirmed. Cross-check agent findings for contradictions. Issue PASS or FAIL.
verifies: all gate claims backed by evidence
set -euo pipefail confirmed on every script

delegates.task_template: Compliance audit for: ${goal}. Scan for PII exposure in log output, error messages, temp files, and debug traces. Verify no secrets or credentials committed (gitleaks full-history scan). Verify scripts do not exfiltrate data to external endpoints without authorization. Verify file permissions: scripts not world-writable, created files use restrictive umask (077). Verify license headers present on all scripts. Check for unauthorized telemetry or analytics. Verify .gitignore excludes .env, credentials, private keys, .bash_history. Flag any compliance violation with regulatory reference and remediation. Issue PASS, FAIL, or WARN verdict.
verifies: no PII in logs or error output
no secrets in git history (gitleaks full scan)
license headers present on all scripts

delegates.task_template: Adversarial review of all gate findings for: ${goal}. Challenge assumptions from every prior phase. For each gate's PASS claim: invert the assumption and test what happens if wrong. Challenge portability claims: does passing on macOS/Linux/Alpine mean truly portable, or are there edge cases with different bash versions (3.2 vs 5.2), different default shells (dash on Ubuntu minimal), or different coreutils (toybox in Android/embedded)? Challenge safety claims: does quoting all variables prevent injection, or are there cases where eval, indirect expansion, or associative array keys bypass quoting? Challenge idempotency claims: does running twice produce the same result, or are there race conditions, lock file issues, or timestamp-dependent behavior? Perform pre-mortem: assume a script caused a production outage 6 months later, enumerate top 5 reasons. Assess blast radius. If all agents agree on PASS, verify it is not groupthink. Issue PROCEED, PROCEED WITH SAFEGUARDS, or RECONSIDER verdict.
verifies: blast radius assessed for remaining risks

phases.checkpoint: Reviewer sign-off, compliance cleared, critic challenge passed
phases.failure_message: Final truth gate failed. Unverified claims, compliance issues, or critic objections detected.
